turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: For example, the Social Insurance Organization, which is one of the largest health insurance companies in Iran, announced that from April to the end of October 2022, 97% of prescriptions of insured patients of this company were issued electronically (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). In addition, electronic health records (EHR) have been set up so far for 98 percent of people in Iran (Rezaei, 2023).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: As an illustration, the Social Insurance Organization, which is among the most prominent healthcare insurance providers in Iran, has revealed that there has been a significant shift towards digital prescriptions for insured patients since April 2022. As of October 2022, the organization reported that 97% of such prescriptions were issued electronically (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). Moreover, electronic health records (EHR) have been implemented for a remarkable 98% of individuals in Iran, according to Rezaei (2023).

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: This study focused on hospitals in Iran, motivated by the growing interest and adoption of digital solutions in Iranian healthcare in response to the Covid-19 pandemic. The pandemic has highlighted the need for efficient and effective digital tools in healthcare, leading to a surge in the adoption of telemedicine, electronic prescriptions, and other digital health solutions. For example, the Social Insurance Organization, which is one of the largest health insurance companies in Iran, announced that from April to the end of October 2022, 97% of prescriptions of insured patients of this company were issued electronically (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). Moreover, electronic health records (EHR) have been implemented for a remarkable 98% of individuals in Iran, according to Rezaei (2023). However, despite the growing interest and adoption of digital transformation in Iranian healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping physical IT infrastructure, which is essential for effective integration and support of digital transformation. These challenges include high costs and inadequate infrastructure. Therefore, understanding the role and mechanism of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation is crucial for effective management of costly IT infrastructure and successful digital transformation in Iranian hospitals.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Motivated by the surge in digital solutions adoption in response to the Covid-19 pandemic, this study aimed to examine the role of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation in hospitals in Iran. Despite the growing interest and adoption of telemedicine, electronic prescriptions, and electronic health records (EHR), hospitals in Iran still face challenges in maintaining and equipping physical IT infrastructure. Such challenges, which include high costs and inadequate infrastructure, pose significant obstacles to effective integration and support of digital transformation. For instance, the Social Insurance Organization, one of the largest healthcare insurance providers in Iran, announced that 97% of prescriptions for insured patients were issued electronically from April to October 2022 (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). Additionally, EHR adoption has been implemented for 98% of individuals in Iran (Rezaei, 2023). Therefore, understanding the role and mechanisms of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation becomes crucial for cost-effective IT infrastructure management and successful digital transformation in Iranian hospitals.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: EDIT: However, despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to implement digital transformation. on the other hand, based on evidence, some hospitals have not been successful in implementing digital transformation despite having a strong physical IT infrastructure. Therefore, understanding the role and mechanism of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation is crucial for Iranian hospitals.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Whilst the adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare sector continues to increase, hospitals are facing significant challenges when it comes to maintaining and equipping physical IT infrastructure to support digital transformation. However, there is also evidence to suggest that some hospitals have struggled to implement digital transformation effectively despite having robust physical IT infrastructure in place. In light of this, it is essential for Iranian hospitals to gain a comprehensive understanding of the role and mechanisms of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation for optimal implementation and successful outcomes.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: However, despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to implement digital transformation. On the other hand, there is also evidence to suggest that some hospitals have struggled to implement digital transformation despite having a strong physical IT infrastructure. In light of this, it is essential for Iranian hospitals to gain a comprehensive understanding of the role and mechanisms of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Although the utilization of digital tools in Iran's healthcare sector has risen, Iranian hospitals encounter issues when it comes to sustaining and equipping expensive physical IT infrastructure to enable digital transformation. Notwithstanding, some hospitals have also experienced difficulties implementing digital transformation effectively, despite having a resilient physical IT infrastructure in place. Consequently, it is vital for Iranian hospitals to obtain an in-depth comprehension of the role and mechanisms of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation to overcome obstacles and achieve successful implementation.
